vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements.description: Provide a fixed-wing UAV center pod with an equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, and motor pylon.
Ensure the motor pod mount is capable of holding a motor with a bolt circle pattern.
Create a cooling inlet path for heat dissipation from the motor pod.
Include a dummy pusher prop mount for future integration.
Design a hinge for the equipment bay door.
Integrate wing-spar reinforcement within the pod.
Provide an access window for maintenance and inspection.
Include a sensor pocket for avionics integration.

generated_parts.label: Center Body Pod
generated_parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

requirements.description: Provide a fixed-wing UAV center pod with an equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, and motor pylon.
Ensure the motor pod mount is capable of holding a motor with a bolt circle pattern.
Create a cooling inlet path for heat dissipation from the motor pod.
Include a dummy pusher prop mount for future integration.
Design a hinge for the equipment bay door.
Integrate wing-spar reinforcement within the pod.
Provide an access window for maintenance and inspection.
Include a sensor pocket for avionics integration.

verify.rule: Ensure all necessary parts and operations are included.
The estimated mass matches the design requirements.

verify.value: All parts and operations are linked to their respective IDs.
verify.rule: Each part and operation should have a unique identifier.

verify.value: Interfaces are reciprocal and clearly defined.
verify.rule: Ensure all interfaces are bidirectionally defined.

verify.value: Visible locking methods and preload evidence are provided.
verify.rule: Critical joints must show locking and preload methods.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on cooling inlet due to direct motor heat.
risk.mit: Implement additional thermal insulation around the cooling inlet.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity at bay door hinge.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness of the bay door hinge area.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly due to complex bolt patterns.
risk.mit: Include alignment pins or guides in the design.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate support structure for motor mount under load.
risk.mit: Add additional stiffeners or reinforce the motor mount area.

risk.desc: Risk of access window obstructing internal components.
risk.mit: Optimize the size and position of the access window.

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage
Service access for battery and sensor modules
Clearance and fit for adjacent subsystems

verify.rule: Each required evidence feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value

verify.value: All parts and features traceable by id/name
verify.rule: part_tree children and geometry_ops must be mutually traceable

verify.value: All interfaces named with exact neighboring subsystem labels
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence on critical joints
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to CFRP material
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies and conduct thermal cycling tests

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and conduct mock-up trials

risk.desc: Possibility of insufficient structural integrity if fasteners are not properly torqued
risk.mit: Specify torque values and implement quality control checks during assembly

risk.desc: Risk of damage during FDM printing process due to support structures
risk.mit: Optimize support structure placement and conduct post-print inspection

risk.desc: Potential for incorrect fit if standard fit vocabulary is not followed
risk.mit: Ensure all fits are named with standard intent and allowances are specified

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Transfers aerodynamic lift and drag loads
Supports wing rotation during takeoff and landing

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus.expected_value: Maintain shape within ±1mm over temperature range

generated_parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
generated_parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

requirements: Provide structural integrity and aerodynamic efficiency
Support control surfaces with proper hinges
Integrate with leading edge sock and wing spars

verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be within budget.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are represented in geometry_ops.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets should map directly to part_tree children and geometry_ops.

verify.value: Interfaces are reciprocal with adjacent modules.
verify.rule: Adjacent interfaces must specify exact neighboring subsystem labels and consistent joint families.

verify.value: Critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to press-fit tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and visual inspection.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity if lightening pockets are oversized.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis to validate structural integrity.

risk.desc: Possibility of thermal expansion affecting fit between EPP foam core and CFRP spar.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion considerations in design and manufacturing processes.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to variations in snap-fit leading edge sock.
risk.mit: Standardize snap-fit dimensions and conduct quality assurance checks.

risk.desc: Potential for hinge line misalignment impacting control surface operation.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment during manufacturing and assembly.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Structural integrity, aerodynamic efficiency, payload distribution

physics_paths.description: Transfers aerodynamic lift forces from wing ribs to the fuselage.
Airflow over the wing rib set generates lift and drag.
Heat from sun exposure is managed by the EPP foam core's insulating properties.

manufacturing_strategy.inspection_implications: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus.description: Ensure the total mass does not exceed the budgeted weight.
Check that all interfaces fit properly without excessive clearance or interference.
Verify that the wing ribs can withstand expected aerodynamic and structural loads.

verify.rule: mass budget
joint evidence

verify.check: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
verify.rule: P0-to-geometry traceability

verify.check: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
verify.rule: coordinate distribution

verify.check: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave where appropriate
verify.rule: non-box feature variety

verify.check: Motion claims are grounded in a named mechanism type with its pivot/axis geometry present
verify.rule: mechanism named not implied

verify.check: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
verify.rule: replaceable module path

verify.check: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
verify.rule: cable harness visibility

verify.check: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
verify.rule: access not just text

verify.check: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius
verify.rule: harness strain relief and bend radius

verify.check: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling
verify.rule: connector accessibility and keying

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on servos due to inadequate heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Increase cooling fin area or add active cooling elements.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to insufficient strain relief at exits.
risk.mit: Add additional strain relief features at critical cable exit points.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Refine manufacturing tolerance to 0.1mm and ensure proper alignment tools.

risk.desc: Risk of improper mating due to lack of clear keying or labeling on connectors.
risk.mit: Implement clear keying or labeling on all connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at snap-fit interfaces due to insufficient clearance.
risk.mit: Increase clearance at snap-fit interfaces to 1mm to ensure robust mating.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels

physics_paths.description: Control horn linkage transfers motion from servo to elevator surface.
Heat dissipation from servo to ambient air.
Power supply from battery to servo.

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile

risk.desc: Potential interference between GPS signal and telemetry data cables.
risk.mit: Ensure proper signal separation and routing.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features at every cable exit.

generated_parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
generated_parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

requirements: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window
Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance
Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window
Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g <= 500

Adjacent-module interfaces and mounting datums named with exact neighboring subsystem labels
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at the wing root due to load path discontinuity.
risk.mit: Ensure continuous load path from wing spar to fuselage frames with appropriate fittings.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal overload on the optical window if airflow management fails.
risk.mit: Implement robust airflow management around the camera bay.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment issues with the spar socket during assembly.
risk.mit: Include clear visual indicators and alignment aids for the spar socket.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

physics_paths.path: Wing spar -> Camera Gimbal Bay -> Optical Window

physics_paths.path: Airflow over wing -> Camera Gimbal Bay -> Airflow out

physics_paths.path: Ambient heat -> Camera Gimbal Bay -> Radiated heat
physics_paths.description: Thermal dissipation from the camera bay.

Data transfer from camera to onboard computer.
